inline_review:
commit 66bc4b81ac9ec3b5414b498ac85c00eb62b48a7d
Author: David Rientjes <rientjes@google.com>

mm, page_alloc: reintroduce page allocation stall warning

This commit reintroduces a warning when a single page allocation takes longer
than reasonably expected (10 seconds). It rate-limits the warning to trigger
only once every 10 seconds system-wide to prevent log spam from concurrent
reclaimers.

> diff --git a/mm/page_alloc.c b/mm/page_alloc.c
> index b1c5430cad4e..230845b9e856 100644
> --- a/mm/page_alloc.c
> +++ b/mm/page_alloc.c
> @@ -282,6 +282,14 @@ EXPORT_SYMBOL(nr_node_ids);
>  EXPORT_SYMBOL(nr_online_nodes);
>  #endif
>  
> +/*
> + * When page allocations stall for longer than a threshold,
> + * ALLOC_STALL_WARN_MSECS, leave a warning in the kernel log.  Only one warning
> + * will be printed during this duration for the entire system.
> + */
> +#define ALLOC_STALL_WARN_MSECS (10 * 1000UL)
> +static unsigned long alloc_stall_warn_jiffies;
> +

[ ... ]

> @@ -4693,6 +4701,40 @@ check_retry_cpuset(int cpuset_mems_cookie, struct alloc_context *ac)
>  	return false;
>  }
>  
> +static void check_alloc_stall_warn(gfp_t gfp_mask, nodemask_t *nodemask,
> +				unsigned int order, unsigned long alloc_start_time)
> +{
> +	static DEFINE_SPINLOCK(alloc_stall_lock);
> +	unsigned long stall_msecs = jiffies_to_msecs(jiffies - alloc_start_time);
> +
> +	if (likely(stall_msecs < ALLOC_STALL_WARN_MSECS))
> +		return;
> +	if (time_before(jiffies, READ_ONCE(alloc_stall_warn_jiffies)))
> +		return;

Since alloc_stall_warn_jiffies is implicitly zero-initialized, and jiffies
starts at INITIAL_JIFFIES (typically 5 minutes before wrap-around), will this
suppress allocation stall warnings during the first 5 minutes of system uptime?

The time_before(jiffies, 0) check will evaluate to true while jiffies is
less than zero (when cast to a signed long), causing check_alloc_stall_warn()
to return early and drop all warnings during early boot.

Should alloc_stall_warn_jiffies be explicitly initialized to INITIAL_JIFFIES?
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"The rate-limiting variable `alloc_stall_warn_jiffies` is implicitly zero-initialized, which silently suppresses allocation stall warnings during the first 5 minutes of system uptime.","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"1. `alloc_stall_warn_jiffies` is declared as a static variable, meaning it defaults to `0`.
2. In the Linux kernel, `jiffies` starts at `INITIAL_JIFFIES` (typically `-300 * HZ`) to intentionally force a wrap-around exactly 5 minutes after boot.
3. The rate-limiting check uses `time_before(jiffies, READ_ONCE(alloc_stall_warn_jiffies))`, which evaluates to `time_before(jiffies, 0)`.
4. The `time_before(a, b)` macro resolves to `(long)(a - b) < 0`. Substituting the values gives `(long)(jiffies - 0) < 0`. 
5. During the first ~5 minutes of uptime, `jiffies` is a large unsigned value that acts as a negative number when cast to a signed `long`. Therefore, the condition evaluates to true.
6. As a result, `check_alloc_stall_warn()` will always return early, completely dropping all page allocation stall warnings during early boot—a critical phase where identifying stalls is often important. The variable should be explicitly initialized to `INITIAL_JIFFIES`."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 66bc4b81ac9ec3b5414b498ac85c00eb62b48a7d
Author: David Rientjes <rientjes@google.com>

mm, page_alloc: reintroduce page allocation stall warning

This commit reintroduces a warning when a single page allocation takes longer
than reasonably expected (10 seconds). It rate-limits the warning to trigger
only once every 10 seconds system-wide to prevent log spam from concurrent
reclaimers.

> diff --git a/mm/page_alloc.c b/mm/page_alloc.c
> index b1c5430cad4e..230845b9e856 100644
> --- a/mm/page_alloc.c
> +++ b/mm/page_alloc.c
> @@ -282,6 +282,14 @@ EXPORT_SYMBOL(nr_node_ids);
>  EXPORT_SYMBOL(nr_online_nodes);
>  #endif
>  
> +/*
> + * When page allocations stall for longer than a threshold,
> + * ALLOC_STALL_WARN_MSECS, leave a warning in the kernel log.  Only one warning
> + * will be printed during this duration for the entire system.
> + */
> +#define ALLOC_STALL_WARN_MSECS (10 * 1000UL)
> +static unsigned long alloc_stall_warn_jiffies;
> +

[ ... ]

> @@ -4693,6 +4701,40 @@ check_retry_cpuset(int cpuset_mems_cookie, struct alloc_context *ac)
>  	return false;
>  }
>  
> +static void check_alloc_stall_warn(gfp_t gfp_mask, nodemask_t *nodemask,
> +				unsigned int order, unsigned long alloc_start_time)
> +{
> +	static DEFINE_SPINLOCK(alloc_stall_lock);
> +	unsigned long stall_msecs = jiffies_to_msecs(jiffies - alloc_start_time);
> +
> +	if (likely(stall_msecs < ALLOC_STALL_WARN_MSECS))
> +		return;
> +	if (time_before(jiffies, READ_ONCE(alloc_stall_warn_jiffies)))
> +		return;

Since alloc_stall_warn_jiffies is implicitly zero-initialized, and jiffies
starts at INITIAL_JIFFIES (typically 5 minutes before wrap-around), will this
suppress allocation stall warnings during the first 5 minutes of system uptime?

The time_before(jiffies, 0) check will evaluate to true while jiffies is
less than zero (when cast to a signed long), causing check_alloc_stall_warn()
to return early and drop all warnings during early boot.

Should alloc_stall_warn_jiffies be explicitly initialized to INITIAL_JIFFIES?

"}
